CS PhD @ University of Minnesota

Building AI agents that reason, adapt, and act.

I work on computer-use agents

I am Shuyu Gan (甘书宇), a Computer Science PhD student working with Minnesota NLP and the Optimization for AI Lab. I am co-advised by Prof. Dongyeop Kang and Prof. Mingyi Hong. My goal is to develop general-purpose agents that can reliably automate complex computer work.

Explore my research ↓ Read my CV ↗
Email Scholar GitHub LinkedIn X / Twitter
AGENTS ALIGNMENT REASONING
researcher.profile ACTIVE
Shuyu Gan Agentic AI Researcher
MSP / CT
COLM '26 LLM personalization
ICLR '25 Agent S
Honorable Mention VISxGenAI 2025
5 works Selected below
01 / Research

From capable models to dependable digital collaborators.

I study the algorithms, feedback signals, and interaction frameworks needed to turn foundation models into agents that can plan, learn, and complete real-world tasks.

01

Agents that use computers

Building multimodal agents that perceive interfaces, reason across applications, and execute long-horizon workflows with less human intervention.

Computer Use Planning Multimodal AI
Featured work: Agent S ↗
02

Reasoning with imperfect feedback

Exploring inference-time scaling and learning signals for tasks where quality is subjective, expensive, or difficult to verify automatically.

Test-Time Scaling Rewards Evaluation
Featured work: Scaling Unverifiable Rewards ↗
03

Alignment through interaction

Designing models that discover latent user preferences, adapt to individual goals, and remain useful and controllable over extended interactions.

LLM Alignment Personalization Human-AI Interaction
Featured work: Latent Preferences ↗
02 / Updates

Signals from the journey.

Recent papers, milestones, and moments worth remembering.

Jump to publications ↓
Apr 2026
Paper

Scaling Unverifiable Rewards was accepted to ACL 2026 Findings.

Aug 2025
Award

A2P-Vis received an Honorable Mention at the VISxGenAI Workshop @ IEEE VIS 2025.

Jan 2025
Paper

Agent S was accepted to ICLR 2025.

Jun 2024
Milestone

Graduated from Zhejiang University and was nominated as an Outstanding Graduate of Zhejiang University.

Jul 2023
Paper

Our work on culture-aware multimodal gesture generation was accepted to ACM Multimedia 2023.

03 / Journey

Learning across labs, disciplines, and ambitious problems.

My path spans agentic AI, multimodal generation, and human-centered systems across UMN, UCSC, UCSD, and Zhejiang University.

Research experience 2023 - Present
Jun 2025 - Present

Graduate Research Assistant

Minnesota NLP & OptimAI Lab, University of Minnesota

Researching agentic AI and inference-time scaling with Prof. Dongyeop Kang and Prof. Mingyi Hong.

Jun 2024 - Nov 2024

Undergraduate Visiting Research Intern

ERIC Lab, UC Santa Cruz

Worked with Prof. Xin Eric Wang on Agent S, an MLLM-based agent for autonomously completing complex computer tasks.

Jul 2023 - Sep 2023

Undergraduate Visiting Research Intern

mlPC Lab, UC San Diego

Studied concept-guided diffusion training with Prof. Zhuowen Tu to improve generation quality and semantic consistency.

Feb 2023 - May 2023

Research Intern

IDEA Lab, Zhejiang University

Explored culture-aware multimodal co-speech gesture generation with Prof. Lingyun Sun.

Education Foundations
2025 - Present

University of Minnesota

PhD in Computer Science

Minneapolis, Minnesota
2020 - 2024

Zhejiang University

Bachelor's in Computer Science and Technology

Turing Class, Chu Kochen Honors College
+

I am grateful to have also been advised by Lingyun Sun, Yi Yang, Zhuowen Tu, and Xin Eric Wang.

04 / Selected work

Research built to move ideas into working systems.

Selected publications across agentic AI, visual insight generation, personalization, and multimodal systems. View Google Scholar ↗

ACL 2026 Findings 2026

Scaling Unverifiable Rewards: A Case Study on Visual Insights

Shuyu Gan, J. Mooney, P. Hao, R. Wang, M. Hong, Q. Wang, D. Kang

Investigating how to scale learning signals for visual insight generation when quality is difficult to verify automatically.

Read paper ↗
Honorable Mention 2025

A2P-Vis: An Analyzer-to-Presenter Agentic Pipeline for Visual Insights Generation and Reporting

Shuyu Gan, R. Wang, J. Mooney, D. Kang

An agentic pipeline that turns visual analysis into structured, presentation-ready insight reports.

Read paper ↗
COLM 2026 2026

Do LLMs Recognize Your Latent Preferences?

I. Tsaknakis, B. Song, Shuyu Gan, D. Kang, A. Garcia, G. Liu, C. Fleming, M. Hong

A benchmark for testing whether language models can uncover latent user preferences through personalized interaction.

Read paper ↗
ICLR 2025 2025

Agent S: An Open Agentic Framework that Uses Computers Like a Human

S. Agashe, J. Han, Shuyu Gan, J. Yang, A. Li, X. E. Wang

An open multimodal agent framework for autonomously performing complex, multi-step computer tasks.

Read paper ↗
ACM MM 2023 2023

Cultural Self-Adaptive Multimodal Gesture Generation Based on Multiple Culture Gesture Dataset

J. Wu, S. Chen, Shuyu Gan, W. Li, C. Yang, L. Sun

Culture-aware co-speech gesture generation grounded in a multi-culture multimodal gesture dataset.

Read paper ↗
05 / Beyond research

A life with games, miles, and fast rallies.

Research is a big part of my world, but not the whole map.

01
</>

Competitive FPS

CS2 and Valorant are my favorite ways to reset, coordinate, and chase one cleaner round.

02
42K

Distance running

I spent three years with ZJU's Kuafu Long Distance Alliance and have raced the 1500m and half marathon.

03
21

Badminton

Still an amateur, still learning, and always happy to play one more game.

Interested in agents that can actually get things done?

I am always glad to discuss research ideas, collaborations, and ambitious agentic systems.
